title: RHEL 8 & podman 2.2.1 - can't run rootless containers when network is attached (rootless-cni-infra)
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I have fresh Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.3 virtual machine with crun and podman installed. I am having trouble  to get any container working when running rootless and specifying the network i.e loading the rootless-cni-infra container

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman network create test --subnet 10.88.8.0/24

2. podman run -d --name nginx --network test -p 8080:80 -p 8443:443 nginx

**Describe the results you received:**
Error: open executable: Permission denied: OCI permission denied

**Describe the results you expected:**
Hash of the started container

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Relevant outputs:
```
[podman@rh8-podman-02 ~]$ uname -a
Linux rh8-podman-02.<redacted> 4.18.0-240.15.1.el8_3.x86_64 #1 SMP Wed Feb 3 03:12:15 EST 2021 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux

[podman@rh8-podman-02 ~]$ cat /etc/os-release
NAME="Red Hat Enterprise Linux"
VERSION="8.3 (Ootpa)"
ID="rhel"
ID_LIKE="fedora"
VERSION_ID="8.3"
PLATFORM_ID="platform:el8"
PRETTY_NAME="Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.3 (Ootpa)"
ANSI_COLOR="0;31"
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:redhat:enterprise_linux:8.3:GA"
HOME_URL="https://www.redhat.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugzilla.redhat.com/"

REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT="Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT_VERSION=8.3
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Red Hat Enterprise Linux"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION="8.3"

[podman@rh8-podman-02 ~]$ grep podman /etc/subuid /etc/subgid
/etc/subuid:podman:165537:65536
/etc/subgid:podman:165537:65536

[podman@rh8-podman-02 ~]$ findmnt -R /sys/fs/cgroup/
TARGET         SOURCE  FSTYPE  OPTIONS
/sys/fs/cgroup cgroup2 cgroup2 rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime,seclabel,nsdelegate

[podman@rh8-podman-02 ~]$ cat /etc/sysctl.d/usrns.conf
user.max_user_namespaces=28633
kernel.unprivileged_userns_clone=1

[podman@rh8-podman-02 ~]$ getenforce
Permissive
```

The exact error I get is:

```
DEBU[0000] rootlessCNIInfraExec: c.ID()=4ad9ade5ad7999c05c42b3bf00622198cb355a3fb8dca76a4bea2e0a203db1c8, config={Command:[rootless-cni-infra alloc b9e08db112ab159b703616b7e05471ee9399cb5b90e1b2f229804656ec40b239 test nginx] Terminal:false AttachStdin:false AttachStdout:false AttachStderr:false DetachKeys:<nil> Environment:map[] Privileged:true User: WorkDir: PreserveFDs:0 ExitCommand:[] ExitCommandDelay:0}, streams={0xc00048e1b0 0xc00048e1d0 <nil> true true false}, end (code=-1, err=[conmon:d]: exec with attach is waiting for start message from parent
[conmon:d]: exec with attach got start message from parent
open executable: Permission denied: OCI permission denied)
``` 

rootless-cni-infra container version is v2.2.1-rhel

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.2.1
API Version:  2
Go Version:   go1.14.7
Built:        Mon Feb  8 23:19:06 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.18.0
  cgroupManager: systemd

  conmon:

  eventLogger: file

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux

  slirp4netns:

  swapFree: 2152460288

    overlay.mount_program:
      Executable: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs

        FUSE library version 3.2.1
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.26

  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-2.2.1-7.module+el8.3.1+9857+68fb1526.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Tested = No, checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide = Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.3 on Hyper-V (Windows 10 Pro)

comments.body: This smell like a crun and/or CNI error somewhere.  @giuseppe any thoughts?

could you try these steps?

```
$ podman unshare
# podman network create test --subnet 10.88.8.0/24
# podman create --name nginx --network test -p 8080:80 -p 8443:443 nginx
# mount=$(podman mount nginx)
# ls -l $mount/usr/sbin/nginx
```

In general, are you able to see files under $mount?

the output from `[root@rh8-podman-02 ~]# ls -l $mount/usr/sbin/nginx ` 
I got is 
`-rwxr-xr-x. 1 root root 1361624 Mar  9 17:52 home/podman/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/41d0646e97006126f22055f7bcc085948b133241ebcc6ba493020766c1c7ff48/merged/usr/sbin/nginx`
Can you add `--log-level=debug` to the `podman run -d` command you use to launch nginx, and provide the output?

Here is the output:
```

It's the rootless CNI infra container, not the main container.
```
DEBU[0000] rootlessCNIInfraExec: c.ID()=df104fc7f338c8c55634e07cdfb90207ae0cdd4e2ecc34d641e517e373c6da0b, config={Command:[rootless-cni-infra alloc 99dece658bb375317221503796eab5b19e1827a11fead77662b81b2c58d917a6 test nginx] Terminal:false AttachStdin:false AttachStdout:false AttachStderr:false DetachKeys:<nil> Environment:map[] Privileged:true User: WorkDir: PreserveFDs:0 ExitCommand:[] ExitCommandDelay:0}, streams={0xc000244f10 0xc000244f20 <nil> true true false}, end (code=-1, err=[conmon:d]: exec with attach is waiting for start message from parent
```

This bit comes immediately before the error and seems a bit suspicious, though this could be from the `log-level=debug` bit being specified.

We could try and dig in further here, but I'm a bit hesitant since https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/9423 seems to be coming along nicely and will yank the problem out at the roots by removing said container entirely.

This is very strange. Podman is installed as instructed and basically  the only thing installed after a clean OS install. How come no-one else got this is beyond me. I won't be keeping my breath while waiting for the mentioned change of loosing the CNI container dependency as I predict it will be a solid three  or four months after the feature is released to hit RHEL 8 main branch. Will continue running rooted until then ...
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since this is fixed in the main branch, I am closing.  This will show up in RHEL 8.4.0.2 some time this summer.
